commits.message:
mit-cut-plan-lsh.3: pacing scale — tempo as named values, not magic numbers

The descent's pacing was ~30 hand-tuned ownVh magic numbers plus a JS PACE
literal and a hardcoded 48vh hold spacer in the build — pacing as folklore,
where 2.2 and 2.4 looked like deliberate distinction but were just noise.

Author a named tempo vocabulary in ONE canonical place, exactly the lsh.1
(motion) / lsh.2 (color) house pattern: a :root PACING SCALE block holds the
scale as unitless tokens (--pace, --tempo-<name>-len/-hold), CSS canonical;
JS reads each back through the guarded cssNum() so a renamed token breaks loud.
Each tempo is a complete shape {len, hold} — scroll length AND trailing linger
collapsed into one named intent. A scene names ONE tempo as data (node.tempo),
the build sizes run + hold from it. The scale ascends like a musical measure:
flit -> pulse -> phrase -> passage -> movement.

Every node retuned by intent against the scale: scenes that should read at the
same speed now share a tempo NAME, not a coincidentally-similar number. Verified
in Chrome: 31/31 nodes on a named tempo, all heights finite, exactly 5 distinct
holds (one per tempo), no console errors; scenes pin/reveal/hold correctly.

[LAW:one-source-of-truth] every pacing scalar lives once, in :root.
[LAW:dataflow-not-control-flow] pacing is a value on the node, not build folklore.
[LAW:one-type-per-behavior] same reading-speed -> same name -> one value.

mit-cut-plan-lsh.2: color story — the accent arc is authored, not incidental (#11)

The palette was a side effect of build order: accentSeq round-robin over a
flat ACCENTS array meant inserting one scene reshuffled every colour
downstream. The journey existed nowhere — it emerged.

Author it as a value carried by the tree, mirroring the lsh.1 house pattern:

- COLOR STORY block in :root names the accent FAMILIES (--acc-iris..ember) —
  vocabulary only, CSS canonical. The hexes live once. [LAW:one-source-of-truth]
- Each node names its family as data (node.accent); the sequence of families
  IS the journey — a deliberate cool-crown-to-warm-core descent (iris surface,
  through the five movements azure→ember, warm at the close). The arc is the
  tree's shape, not the loop's counter. [LAW:dataflow-not-control-flow]
- The shared #211c2e radial on .content is now derived from var(--accent) via
  color-mix — every scene's field is one rule keyed to its own accent, never a
  second authored colour. [LAW:single-enforcer]
- JS reads families back through the guarded cssVar() (its message generalised
  now that colour shares the enforcer with motion); a missing/misspelled accent
  breaks the build loud, so the arc can't silently gap. [LAW:no-silent-failure]
- The explode map's five movement chips are tinted the family each section
  wears below — the slab previews the whole descent, the spine mit-story-5rm.4's
  thread will gather.

accentSeq and the round-robin fallback are gone. Verified in Chrome: 31 nodes,
31 authored accents resolving iris·6 → azure·3 → teal·2 → jade·9 → amber·4 →
ember·7, zero console errors, the field washes reading as a deliberate
progression from violet through to ember.
